name: reacTable
organization: Music Technology Group, Universitat Pompeu Fabra
short_description: A round, multi-user tangible tabletop instrument where physical pucks are arranged to patch a modular synthesizer in real time.
long_description: The reacTable is a translucent round table on which performers place and rotate physical objects (tangibles) that represent the modules of a modular synthesizer; their positions, orientations, and proximities dynamically patch and parameterize the audio graph, with the connections and waveforms projected back onto the table surface. A camera beneath the table tracks fiducial markers on the underside of each puck via the reacTIVision computer-vision system, communicated through the TUIO protocol. It is a landmark tangible user interface for collaborative live music, demonstrating how shared, graspable physical objects can make a complex generative system legible and playable by multiple people at once.
ai_note: The system uses fiducial-marker computer vision (reacTIVision) and audio synthesis rules; no machine learning or AI model runs inside it.

images.caption: A reacTable in use, showing tangible pucks and projected synth connections on the round translucent surface (Altman Center, 2007).
images.alt_text: A glowing round translucent table in a dark room with several small physical blocks arranged on its surface and bright projected lines connecting them, with a person's hands interacting.

sources.title: The reacTable: exploring the synergy between live music performance and tabletop tangible interfaces
Reactable - Music Technology Group, Universitat Pompeu Fabra
sources.publisher: Music Technology Group, Universitat Pompeu Fabra

sources.title: reacTIVision: a computer-vision framework for table-based tangible interaction

interactions.title: Rotate a puck to bend its pitch
interactions.description: A performer twists an oscillator puck by hand and its frequency slides up or down in real time, with the change shown as a tightening or loosening waveform projected around the object.

interactions.title: Patch a synth by sliding objects together
interactions.description: Dropping a filter puck between an oscillator and the central output makes the connections snap into place automatically — the system links the oscillator into the filter and the filter into the output purely from proximity, drawing bright lines between them.

interactions.title: Park an LFO nearby to add wobble
interactions.description: Setting a low-frequency oscillator puck close to a sound generator starts modulating it; nudging it nearer or farther changes how strongly the modulation bites, so vibrato and tremolo become a matter of where you place an object on the table.

interactions.title: Draw a level with your fingertip
interactions.description: Touching the glowing halo that surrounds a puck and dragging a finger around it sets the amplitude of that voice, mixing tangible objects and direct multi-touch on the same lit surface.

interactions.title: Lock everything to a scale with the tonalizer
interactions.description: Placing the tonalizer puck constrains the oscillators on the table to a chosen musical scale, while a sequencer or radar puck spins as a periodic trigger so non-musicians can keep a patch sounding tuneful and in time.

interactions.title: Several hands jamming on one table
interactions.description: Because the round surface has no front or privileged seat, multiple performers stand around it and rearrange each other's pucks at once, building and dismantling one shared audio graph collaboratively.

interactions.title: Björk plays it live on the Volta tour
interactions.description: Björk made the reacTable a centerpiece instrument of her 2007 Volta world tour, debuting it on stage at Coachella on April 27, 2007 and using it through songs like "Declare Independence."

interactions.title: A museum floor that teaches synthesis by touch
interactions.description: Visitors with no studio training rearrange a handful of pucks and immediately hear cause and effect — move the filter closer, the sound brightens — turning an exhibit into a hands-on lesson in how a modular synth is wired.

interactions.title: Tabletop scoring for a tangible game
interactions.description: Game tokens that already carry fiducial markers could double as sound objects on the same surface, so where a player parks a piece on the board also shapes the live soundtrack — grounded in the table's existing puck-tracking and proximity rules.
